cmad.notes: No single observatory-level CMAD exists for SDO. CMADs are maintained per instrument (e.g. the SDO-EVE Calibration and Measurement Algorithms Document, led by T. N. Woods; https://soma.larc.nasa.gov/lws/pdf_files/4.2a%20CMAD%20SDO-EVE.pdf), which corroborate the instrument PIs found via the SPASE instrument records but provide no unified observatory team front page. The SDO Senior Review proposals (e.g. 2020; http://hmi.stanford.edu/papers/SDO_SR_2020_assembled_3.pdf) name the current project team led by W. Dean Pesnell.

evidence.source: Lead (1st) author of the SDO mission overview paper 'The Solar Dynamics Observatory (SDO)' (Pesnell, Thompson & Chamberlin, 2012, Solar Physics 275:3 — non-alphabetized)

notes: The SDO Observatory SPASE record is sparse: its only Contact is W. Dean Pesnell (ProjectScientist) and its only link is the NASA/GSFC SDO web page. It contains NO InstrumentID links, OperatingSpan, RevisionHistory, or PublicationInfo. The three instruments were reached via their conventional SPASE IDs spase://SMWG/Instrument/SDO/{AIA,EVE,HMI} and confirmed against the provider instruments page; consider adding these InstrumentIDs to the Observatory record downstream.

Instrument PIs (Woods/EVE, Scherrer/HMI, Title+Jin+Cheung+Schrijver/AIA) are carried forward as Strong because they are named PI/Former-PI in the instrument SPASE records and (for Woods, Scherrer, Title) on the provider instruments page. The downstream role-assignment agent should decide whether instrument PIs belong on the Observatory record or only on their respective instrument records.

AIA shows a PI succession in its SPASE record: Alan M. Title (original) -> Karel Schrijver -> Mark C. M. Cheung -> Meng Jin (current). All four are included; activity-window ordering is left to the downstream agent.

Barbara J. Thompson and Phillip C. Chamberlin are promoted to Medium/included: they are the 2nd and 3rd authors of the non-alphabetized mission overview paper (first 3 authors qualify per the paper-selection heuristic) AND are the two SDO Deputy Project Scientists. (A prior run had excluded them as non-lead co-authors; that was inconsistent with the non-alphabetized first-3-authors rule.) Their Deputy-PS role is arguably observatory-Contact-level (Strong) but is absent from the record's current Contacts, so kept at Medium.

Mission overview paper used: 'The Solar Dynamics Observatory (SDO)', Pesnell, Thompson & Chamberlin, Solar Physics 2012, DOI 10.1007/s11207-011-9841-3 — within the expected 2-5 year window of the launch (11 Feb 2010). Used only for lead/first-author selection, not for dumping co-authors.
No SMWG Person record was found for Phillip C. Chamberlin (404 at Phillip.C.Chamberlin, Philip.C.Chamberlin, and Phillip.Chamberlin); person_record left null. Verify spelling downstream if promoted.

No observatory-level CMAD exists; per-instrument CMADs (notably SDO-EVE, led by Woods) corroborate the EVE PI. Crossref returned no ORCIDs/affiliations for the mission paper, so author ORCIDs are null except Jin (0000-0002-9672-3873) and Cheung (0000-0003-2110-9753), which were captured from their SMWG Person records.
